result.summary: Back4app exposes a Parse REST API at `https://parseapi.back4app.com`, a Parse GraphQL API at `https://parseapi.back4app.com/graphql`, a stdio MCP server via `@back4app/mcp-server-back4app`, and the `b4a` CLI; authenticate app APIs with Parse app headers (`X-Parse-Application-Id` plus REST API key, Client Key, or for some admin/introspection uses Master Key) and authenticate MCP/CLI with a Back4app account key/personal access token.

parse_app_rest_key.label: Parse app credentials for REST/GraphQL/API console (Application ID plus app key such as REST API key or Client Key; some admin/introspection actions use Master Key)
parse_app_rest_key.setup: Create a Back4app app, then open [Parse Dashboard](https://parse-dashboard.back4app.com/) for that app. The docs say to use `Core` → `API Console` → `GraphQL Console`, and that the generated [API Reference](https://www.back4app.com/docs/parse-dashboard/api-reference) exposes your app keys. Use the key pair required by your action: `X-Parse-Application-Id` + `X-Parse-REST-API-Key` for REST requests; `X-Parse-Application-Id` + `X-Parse-Client-Key` for GraphQL client access; `X-Parse-Application-Id` + `X-Parse-Master-Key` for admin/schema retrieval shown in Apollo examples.

back4app_account_key.label: Back4app account key / personal access token
back4app_account_key.setup: Log into your Back4app account, hover over the `Hello, [username]` menu, open `Account Keys`, and create a new key. The MCP docs recommend naming it for its purpose (for example, `AI Assistant MCP`) and copying it securely because you won't be able to see it again. The same token is also used by the Back4app CLI setup via `b4a configure accountkey`. See [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://www.back4app.com/docs/mcp) and [Command Line Interface Setup](https://www.back4app.com/docs/local-development/parse-cli).
